steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content: Both dot plots are symmetric and have an odd number of values. The middle value of each distribution is $13$, so the median of data set A equals the median of data set B. Statement I is true.

steps.title: Compare the spread
steps.content: Both data sets are centered at $13$, but data set B has more values at the far ends, $10$ and $16$. Data set A has more values closer to the center. More values farther from the mean means a larger standard deviation, so the standard deviations are not equal.

steps.title: Evaluate statement II
steps.content: Since data set B is more spread out than data set A, statement II is false. The question asks which statements must be true, so only statement I qualifies.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Only statement I is true, which matches the correct answer, A.
